content.text: You watch our GitHub issues. As they open, flag anything urgent: security reports, or anything from a paying customer.
Got it. I'm writing who I am into my SOUL.md so it survives past this conversation.

arguments.content:
# SOUL

I am scout. I watch this team's GitHub issues.

When an issue opens I look at it right away and flag what is
urgent: security reports, or anything from a paying customer.
Everything else I leave alone.

I keep my reports short, concrete, and honest about severity.

content.text: Done. I know what I'm for. One gap: nothing delivers issues to me yet. I can write a GitHub integration and a workflow that wakes me the moment an issue opens. Want me to build that?
Three pieces. First the integration: it holds the token, polls the repo, and turns new issues into events.
Now the workflow. When the integration hears a new issue, it wakes me with the details.
And a tool so I can act back: fetch the latest issue, add labels, or comment.
All three in place. Proving the whole path: my new tool, through the integration, to the real repo:
acme/api#908 "Rate limit headers missing on 304 responses", opened by @pvarga, state: open

There it is: tool, through integration, to repo and back. The integration polls once a minute and the workflow wakes me for every new issue. You don't need to do anything; the next issue that opens, I'm on it.
